differential_diagnoses: Primary Angiitis of the Central Nervous System (PACNS)
next_steps: Magnetic resonance angiography (MRA) and high-resolution vessel wall imaging
Repeat lumbar puncture with cytology and extensive infectious/inflammatory panel
Whole-body PET/CT scan to evaluate for occult malignancy (e.g., melanoma)
Genetic testing for familial CAA (e.g., APP, PSEN1, PSEN2 mutations)
Consider diagnostic trial of high-dose corticosteroids if inflammatory pathology is suspected
